title: panicum philadelphicum subsp. lithophilum
intro: Panicum philadelphicum subsp. lithophilum, also known as Philadelphia panic grass, is a type of plant species in the Poaceae family. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Panicum philadelphicum subsp. lithophilum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its roots, stems, and leaves, which are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
